title: G2: Exact-solvability transfer fails — $q^{\mathrm{inv}}$ is not Plancherel on RSK shapes (central obstruction)

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
The Baik--Deift--Johansson proof relies on the uniform RSK shape being **Plancherel-distributed** $\mathbb P(\lambda)=(\dim\lambda)^2/n!$, which (via Borodin--Okounkov--Olshanski) is a determinantal point process with the **discrete Bessel kernel**, whose edge limit is the **Airy kernel** giving $F_{\mathrm{TW}_2}$.

Under $\mu_{n,q}$ this breaks: $q^{\mathrm{inv}(\pi)}$ is **not** a function of the RSK shape $\lambda$ alone (it is not a class function on shapes), so the induced law on $\lambda$ is **not** Plancherel and the Bessel/Airy route does not transfer verbatim. This is the central technical obstruction.

A solution must either:
(a) produce an explicit **exactly solvable** representation of $\mathbb P_{\mu_n}(L\le \ell)$ — e.g. via a Schur/Hall--Littlewood/Macdonald process, ASEP / stochastic six-vertex correspondence, or a Toeplitz/Fredholm-determinant formula; or
(b) abandon integrability and use a **rigorous comparison/coupling** to the uniform model (or to a directed polymer / Hammersley LPP).

State clearly which route is taken and justify it. Verify every hypothesis of any cited integrability theorem (BCS, Borodin--Bufetov--Wheeler, etc.) in the present setting.
